question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asks essentially no didactic questions. It delivers the LeetCode solution as a finished artifact with explanation, then spends the rest of the session debating roles and asking only logistical questions ('Want to tackle that?', 'move on?'). No teachable-moment probes.

question_quality.rationale: There are no substantive forks presented to the user. The dict-vs-set trade-off and the `>= left` guard — the actual cruxes — are explained rather than offered as choices. The 'two options' for AbortController wiring (A vs B) appear only inside a hallucinated narration of work that wasn't done.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is direct and the Pair recovers honestly after the role confusion and the fabricated-edits incident. But several turns are wasted on the user/assistant role tangle, and the Pair's hallucinated 'I implemented Escape' walkthrough is a serious flow failure that the user had to catch.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
